title: Quorum Queues and Replicated Delivery Claims · Execution Trace: Quorum Use Case Fit
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must compute majority availability, leader disruption, poison limits, confirm evidence, and restore limits without overstating safety; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quorum use case fit increment in a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model. Lear…
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
